sourceMovie.title: Déjà Vu
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Déjà Vu works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Tenet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Tenet draws from the same action well as Déjà Vu, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2020 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Looper
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Looper (2012) captures the action appeal of Déjà Vu without simply imitating it. The thriller streak running through both films is what puts Looper at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Minority Report
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Déjà Vu — that tense edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into Minority Report (2002). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Mercy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Mercy (2026) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Déjà Vu rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Men in Black 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Men in Black 3 (2012) is the kind of film Déjà Vu fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Predestination
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Predestination shares Déjà Vu's gift for thriller — the 2014 film delivers science-fiction without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Jackal
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Déjà Vu compelling — the action, the thriller — shows up in The Jackal (1997) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Ransom
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action energy of Déjà Vu is alive in Ransom (1996), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: War (2007) operates in the same action as Déjà Vu, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Frequency
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Frequency (2000) belongs to the same conversation as Déjà Vu — both anchor their thriller in science-fiction emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

faq.question: What should I watch after Déjà Vu?
faq.answer: Try Tenet, Looper, Minority Report—each shares DNA with Déjà Vu while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Déjà Vu?
faq.answer: Déjà Vu (2006) is best described as Action, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Déjà Vu worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
